definitions: To try to communicate with someone who is unresponsive or unwilling to listen.
To make a request or argument to someone who ignores it completely.
examples: I tried to explain my side, but it was like talking to a brick wall.
Arguing with him about politics is like talking to a brick wall; he never changes his mind.
She felt like she was talking to a brick wall when she tried to get her kids to clean their rooms.
synonyms: banging your head against a brick wall
